name: Philodendron gloriosum 'Tricolor'
origin: Colombia; variegated collector form in cultivation
aboutText: Philodendron gloriosum 'Tricolor' is an exceptionally beautiful terrestrial aroid native to Colombia. Growing on a crawling surface rhizome, it produces massive heart-shaped velvet leaves with prominent white venation. The 'Tricolor' mutation features gorgeous green, yellow-cream, and soft pink marbled variegation. It thrives in highly organic, well-draining soil and shaded bright indirect light.

morphology.leafShape: Broad velvety heart-shaped with deep sinus
morphology.variegation: Tricolor variegation in cream, yellow, and soft pink marbling
morphology.growthHabit: Terrestrial growth via thick surface rhizome

fieldNotes.title: The Creeping Masterpiece in Pink and Gold
fieldNotes.author: Ariod Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Philodendron gloriosum is one of the most famous terrestrial aroids, creeping along the forest floor of Colombia on a thick horizontal rhizome. The 'Tricolor' cultivar is a masterclass in somatic mutation, displaying green, cream-yellow, and soft pink variegation. Unlike most climbers, gloriosum crawls, and its large, velvety leaves are highly sensitive to soil depth. The tricolor variegation is highly sought after and requires rich, organic, well-draining soil and shaded bright indirect light to maintain its colorful display.
